text.p: I write to confirm the support of the Department of English of this university for the programme of your study visit to the university from March 1st to March 12th 2010. Your programme of visit is very much in line with the desire of our university for collaboration and exchange programme between staff and students of different universities.

It will be our greatest desire to receive your students and let them share knowledge and interact with our students here. We shall be ready to provide the logistic support necessary for the visit. A step by step response in italics to the programme of your study visit is presented below:

p.italics: As soon as we receive your flight schedule, a staff of the Department of English (host department) whom you will recognize easily will be detailed to receive you at the Airport in Lagos on your arrival. Please, make provision for a night lodging at a nearby hotel to the Airport (say about $50 per room which may allow pairing) if you are arriving after 4pm.

text.p: March 2: Students get to know each other; German students learn about studying English in Ife, possibly visit some lectures.

p.italics: A 3 hour symposium (9am - 12noon) will be organized by the host department to address the topic. At least two staff members, one graduate and one undergraduate student will be lead paper presenters. The university community will be invited via e-mail.

Arrangement will be made for your visiting students to partake in lecture sessions with host students or students in a nearby university (not more than 1 hour journey by road transport), as the case may be.

text.p: March 3: German students present the purpose of the data collection, their interview questions and questionnaires, Nigerian students comment on the suitability and suggest changes, if necessary.

p.italics: This can be accommodated in the graduate seminar of the host department which holds normally on Friday 9am-12 noon every week. The time schedule may change to suit the programme of the visitors.

text.p: March 4-6: Recordings of sociolinguistic interviews with Nigerians of various age groups; interviews carried out in pairs, i.e. one Nigerian and one German student.

p.italics: There will be no problem regarding this. The university community (which is residential to students and a large percentage of staff) and adjoining town(s) are extensive enough for field work and should provide enough data required. The supporting group of students will be provided but may need to be paid some stipends.

text.p: March 7: Visiting Ile-Ife and introduction to Yoruba culture

p.italics: The host department will request resource persons from both the Department of Linguistics and African Languages and Institute of Cultural Studies in our university to arrange a 2hr lecture sessions for our visitors and this will be followed by a visit to areas of interest in Ile-Ife.

text.p: March 8-10: Carrying out questionnaire on acceptability of certain grammatical structures.

p.italics: Respondents are available to researchers to the extent of their demand and there should be no problem at all about this activity.

text.p: March 11: Visiting some of the surrounding/towns

p.italics: The visitors will definitely enjoy a visit to a number of places of historic/cultural significance, e.g. Ife Museum; the palace of the Ooni (Paramount ruler) of Ife, a city regarded historically as the cradle of the Yoruba race; Olumirin waterfall at Erin-Jesa etc.

text.p: I hereby officially invite you to our department and am awaiting your arrival in March 2010. I assure your team of a wonderful period of academic fulfillment and relaxation. Please, let us know as soon as your application for funding is approved so that we can begin to make the necessary preparations regarding the visit.
